title: Fluentd Tag Routing Misconfiguration — Unmatched Records and Null Route
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "pattern not matched" OR "no patterns matched" OR "record is suppressed" OR "disable_router" OR "unmatched"
| rex field=_raw "tag=(?<tag>[\\w._-]+)"
| stats count, values(tag) as tags by host, config_file
| where count >= 20
| sort -count
description: After adding new container namespaces or syslog facilities, tags often fail to match `<match>` stanzas, silently sending events to the wrong store or dropping them. Log signatures for unmatched tags expose config gaps quickly.
value: Stops blind spots when Kubernetes labels change or legacy apps rotate facility codes.
implementation: Augment with a metrics counter from `fluent-plugin-prometheus` for `router_no_match`. Map `config_file` to Git repo paths. Alert on new `tag` values appearing in this search.
visualization: Treemap (host × tag), table of top unmatched tags, diff vs previous week.
grandmaExplanation: We read the forwarder and buffer logs for stress and give-ups so we see log loss risk before disks or patience run out.
knownFalsePositives: Buffer pain from downstream saturation, bad TLS, or local disk. We check agent and destination before blind buffer tuning.
